title_of_original: State Aid SA.115160 (2024/NN) - Germany Measures concerning Haitec AG, Flughafen Frankfurt Hahn GmbH, and Ryanair
case_description: [1] Fly Smooth Airline alleges that the Region of Upper Austria established a special-purpose entity in 2009, called Upper Austria Development GmbH (UAD), [2] with the aim of acquiring and marketing land parcels for the benefit of the regional airport operator, Upper Austria Airport GmbH (UAA). [3] According to Fly Smooth, the Region injected EUR 12 million in equity into UAD, [4] with a broader plan to invest up to EUR 48.5 million in land acquisition and development. [5] Austria confirmed that UAD was incorporated on 7 May 2009,[6]  with a total planned investment of up to EUR 50 million. [7] Its shareholders include the Region of Upper Austria (62%), other public shareholders (6%), and private investors (32%), consisting of three land development companies. [8] On 30 April 2009, the Region’s Ministry of Economic Affairs and the private investors signed a formal agreement to establish UAD. [9] This agreement set out the intention to create the company jointly, [10] to invest in airport-linked land on equal commercial terms, [11] and to share both risks and returns. [12] All participants assumed that land development near the airport had strong commercial potential, [13] provided air traffic continued to grow. [14] On the date of incorporation, all shareholders made their capital contributions simultaneously. [15] The Region contributed EUR 9 million, [16] which was financed from the regional budget. [17] Each shareholder group participated in governance through board representation, [18] with the private investors appointing two of six directors. [19] All major investment decisions and contractual negotiations were taken jointly. [20] In 2009, UAD concluded a contract with UAA [21] granting it an exclusive option to purchase specific land plots owned by UAA. [22] The total agreed payment for the option was EUR 9 million, [23] split over two years: EUR 5.1 million in 2009 and EUR 3.9 million in 2010, in line with the contractual terms. [24] The option contract was negotiated and approved by the board of UAD, [25] with the participation of both public and private directors. [26] The agreed payments in 2009 and 2010 were made from UAD's budget. [27] The contract was terminated on 31 December 2010, [28] following a joint reassessment by the shareholders. [29] The decision was based on the deterioration in the economic outlook caused by the 2008–2009 financial crisis, [30] which undermined earlier expectations about airport-driven land value appreciation. [31] Following its preliminary examination, the Commission reviewed an independent land valuation report dated June 2009, [32] which estimated the market value of the exclusive option rights at approximately EUR 9 million. [33] The valuation was prepared by a certified property consultancy and took into account regional transaction data, land use restrictions, and anticipated appreciation linked to airport development.
gold_parsing: intervention_decided_at_same_time('buying option', 'Region of Upper Austria', 'private shareholder'). % [[19];[25]
intervention_carried_at_same_time('buying option', 'Region of Upper Austria', 'private shareholder'). % [25]
same_terms_and_conditions('buying option', 'Region of Upper Austria', 'private shareholder'). % [11],[12]
